title: Saudi SFDA / MEWA Halal Protocol for Food & Livestock Imports

halal_center_platform.name: Saudi Halal Center (SHC) Portal
halal_center_platform.description: All facilities exporting halal products to Saudi Arabia must register on the Saudi Halal Platform to obtain Halal Facility Certificates (HFC) and Halal Shipment Certificates (HSC).

facility_certificate_annual.notes: Plus VAT. Optional Saudi Halal Center Logo usage fee: 6,000 SAR per product per year.

shipment_certificate.notes: Additional charges apply for heavier increments. Plus VAT.

haccp_requirements.title: Health conditions in food factories and their workers
haccp_requirements.scope: All abattoirs, poultry slaughterhouses, and food factories preparing meat of any type must fully implement HACCP across all production stages.

licensing_steps: Obtain environmental permit from National Center for Environmental Compliance Monitoring
Establish and equip facility per SFDA technical guides and licensing entity requirements (Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs, Industrial/Economic Cities Authority, or Royal Commission)
Obtain HACCP certificate from accredited certification body
Conduct laboratory testing in SFDA-approved private laboratory
Register in unified electronic system 'Ghad' and submit operating license request
Obtain final operating license approval from SFDA General Authority for Food and Drug
required_documents: Industrial license copy from Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources
Lease agreement (if renting warehouse; minimum 20 sqm for food storage)

facility_design.size_and_area: Must be spacious and proportional to SFDA-approved factory construction plan; include production halls, warehouses, staff quarters, laboratory, maintenance, and administrative offices.
facility_design.equipment_spacing: Equipment at least 2 meters from walls; adjacent equipment at least 4 meters apart.
facility_design.materials: Corrosion-resistant, non-absorbent, easy to clean and sterilize.
facility_design.temperature_control: Suitable temperature-controlled handling and storage with monitoring and recording.
facility_design.ventilation: Prevent airflow from contaminated to clean areas; regular filter maintenance.
facility_design.sanitation: Adequate flush lavatories, hand wash basins with hot/cold water and soap, separate from food washing facilities.

staffing_requirements.health_certificates: All workers must have valid health certificates from competent authority.
staffing_requirements.medical_examination: Periodic medical exams at least annually; pre-resumption checks after vacations abroad.
staffing_requirements.exclusion: Workers with contaminated wounds, sores, or contagious diseases excluded until treated.
staffing_requirements.first_aid: Available on site with trained responders.
staffing_requirements.quality_department: Preferably implement a quality/control system with qualified personnel for self-monitoring, inspection, and audits.
staffing_requirements.food_safety_system: Preferably adopt a food safety system (e.g., ISO 22000 / FSSC 22000).

facility_certification_requirements.inspection: SFDA Designated Officer conducts on-site visit before license issuance. License valid for one year; can be suspended or withdrawn for violations.

labeling_requirements.halal_mark_rules: Prepackaged food labels must not include any halal claims, symbols, logos, or marks unless authenticating evidence is provided from accredited bodies designated by the Saudi Halal Center.
labeling_requirements.carcass_labeling: For carcasses and wholesale meat, a durable label containing primary GSO data must be securely affixed via wire to one end of the carcass.

livestock_import_requirements.authority: Ministry of Environment, Water and Agriculture (MEWA)
livestock_import_requirements.import_permit: Required from MEWA Animal and Plant Quarantine Department before shipments leave exporting country.
livestock_import_requirements.health_certificate: Must be from recognized authority in country of origin, duly authenticated by Saudi embassy in that country.
livestock_import_requirements.direct_route: Must ship directly from country of origin to KSA without anchorage or call to any banned/infected port or country.
livestock_import_requirements.pre_export_quarantine: Livestock must undergo strict pre-export quarantine at certified facilities (e.g., Berbera Port managed by Saudi-Emirates International Veterinary Quarantine Management Company for Somaliland exports).
livestock_import_requirements.disease_inspection: Inspected for epidemic diseases (Rift Valley Fever, Foot and Mouth Disease, etc.). Necessary vaccinations required.
livestock_import_requirements.rejection_threshold: Whole shipment rejected if infected with diseases listed in Table 1 (e.g., FMD, RVF, rabies, BSE).
livestock_import_requirements.quarantine_threshold: Shipment may be quarantined if infection rate does not exceed 10% of random sample for diseases in Table 2. If >10%, shipment refused entry.
livestock_import_requirements.banned_countries_history: Saudi Arabia banned live animal imports from Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, Sudan, Kenya, and Djibouti at end of 1990s. Djibouti barred in 2019 after RVF-positive sample.

recent_imports_ethiopia.2020_04: 3,050 sheep from Ethiopia arrived at Al-Ahsa Regional Airport; physical and lab tests confirmed healthy and disease-free.

ban_status_ethiopia.note: The Reporter Ethiopia reported in March 2022 that the year-long ban on Ethiopian livestock imports remained in place, forcing diplomatic efforts. Contradicts 2020 MEWA import data—suggest ban may be intermittent or lifted and reimposed.

ban_status_somaliland.source: Somaliland Minister of Livestock and Fisheries confirmed lifting of 11-year ban.
ban_status_somaliland.quarantine: Animals quarantined up to two weeks at Berbera port before export.

frozen_meat.temperature: Not exceeding -18°C continuously until reaching consumer
frozen_meat.shelf_life_from_slaughter: Shipment must arrive within 4 months of slaughter

transit_and_storage_requirements.container_requirements: Shipped in closed, sealed transshipment containers waxed with red wax or lead. Containers must not be opened or exposed to harsh weather at transit stations.
transit_and_storage_requirements.third_country_processing: No repacking, cutting, canning, or packaging permitted in third countries.
transit_and_storage_requirements.viscera: Shipped separately from meat containers; accompanied by health certificate confirming healthy origin and veterinary inspection.

certification_failure_and_appeal.rejection_authority: SFDA Border Inspection Posts (BIPs) and Executive Management of Food Import Control (EDIFC)
rejection_reasons: Non-compliance with GSO or Saudi regulations/standards
Missing health certificate or improper authentication
certification_failure_and_appeal.rejected_consignments: Must be re-exported or destroyed under SFDA supervision.

appeal_process.timeline: File written appeal within 60 days of refusal decision.
appeal_process.submission: Written appeal to EDIFC at SFDA headquarters, with certified letter from Chamber of Commerce and complete shipment documents.
appeal_process.review: EDIFC forwards to SFDA special committee which studies shipment documentation and BIP test results.
appeal_process.outcome: If BIP action found in error, EDIFC overturns and instructs clearance. If compliant with rules, rejection stands.
importer_obligations: Bear costs of any new laboratory tests if re-testing approved
Responsible for value of goods if shipment remains at port during appeal
For frozen/chilled shipments, provide temperature reader from rejection until final decision
appeal_process.repeat_appeals: Appeal cannot be repeated for same customs declaration/consignment to same SFDA branch.

certification_failure_and_appeal.clearance_timeline: Containers can be cleared in less than five working days if all documents are in order and products meet Saudi/Gulf specifications.

precedents_for_ethiopia_somaliland_lives: April 2020: MEWA announced 27,827 head of cattle (sheep, cows, camels) from Djibouti, Ethiopia, and Romania arrived in KSA.
April 2020: 3,050 sheep from Ethiopia arrived at Al-Ahsa Regional Airport; passed physical and lab tests.
March 2022: Somaliland government confirmed lifting of 11-year livestock export ban to Saudi Arabia; Djibouti and Somalia also included.
March 2022: The Reporter Ethiopia reported that Saudi Arabia's year-long ban on Ethiopian livestock imports remained in place, suggesting intermittent or re-imposed restrictions.
gaps: Exact current ban status for Ethiopian live animal exports to Saudi Arabia (conflicting reports between 2020 imports and 2022 ban).
Specific facility size minimums for meat processing plants (beyond general proportional requirements).
Detailed list of SFDA-recognized private laboratories for testing in Ethiopia/Somaliland.
Appeal success rates and average resolution timelines.
Current live animal quarantine facility capacity and locations in Ethiopia.
Updated 2024-2025 MEWA circulars on banned countries and disease status.
Exact cost of environmental permits and municipal licenses for foreign facilities.
